title: Keep the notes list up to date across sessions
summary: When your notes change somewhere else, the list updates on its own so you always see the latest version without reloading the page.
motivation: Use the existing authenticated note-events stream so the notes list refreshes after backend note changes from another session.

included: GET /api/notes/events remains the backend Server-Sent Events contract
the frontend opens the stream with authenticated fetch, not native EventSource, because bearer headers are required
note created/updated/deleted events trigger a debounced notes refetch
stream teardown runs on component unmount and when authentication is lost
excluded: changing the backend SSE endpoint to query-token or cookie auth
optimistic event payload application; the frontend refetches canonical notes instead
collaborative editing, conflict resolution, or per-note live cursors

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Listen for note changes as they happen
work_items.summary: Open a signed-in live connection that listens for notes being added, changed, or removed.

component_map.notes: Use browser fetch, ReadableStream, TextDecoder, and AbortController; no native EventSource because headers are needed.

behavior: opens GET /notes/events with the same bearer and one-refresh retry policy used by authenticated non-JSON requests
parses SSE data frames containing JSON note event payloads and ignores heartbeat/comment/blank frames
uses AbortController for explicit teardown and component unmount
does not add a new dependency or change backend auth semantics

signals: explicit-open-question:uses fetch streaming instead of native EventSource because Authorization headers are required

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/http.test.ts src/services/noteEvents.test.ts src/features/notes/useNoteEvents.test.ts
assertions: The stream client sends Authorization and aborts cleanly.
SSE data frames are parsed into note event objects; heartbeat frames are ignored.
A 401 before the stream opens uses the existing one-refresh retry path exactly once.

work_items.title: Refresh notes from live events
work_items.summary: Reload the notes list when a change comes in, and keep the note you have open in sync.

component_map.notes: No new visible UI; refresh keeps sidebar and active note in sync with backend events.

behavior: AppShell starts the note-events stream after the initial notes load while authenticated
created, updated, and deleted event payloads schedule one debounced fetchNotes call
deleted active notes clear or reselect consistently after the refetched list returns
stream errors do not clear the visible notes list; they may expose a non-blocking status for tests
teardown happens on component unmount and when auth becomes unauthenticated

signals: capability-or-resource:AppShell opens a long-lived backend stream
not-computed:e2e multi-tab behavior beyond unit stream simulation

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/features/notes/useNoteEvents.test.ts src/features/notes/useNotes.test.ts src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts
assertions: AppShell starts the event stream after initial fetch and aborts it on unmount.
Multiple quick note events cause a debounced single refetch.
A deleted active note is reconciled against the refetched list without leaving a broken activeId.
